_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify, locate, and track targets via geospatial analysis techniques.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of computer vision task taxonomy, including image classification, object detection, segmentation, pose estimation, and generation, and the architectures appropriate for each.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-014 (AI, cosine: 0.278), AI-S-010 (AI, cosine: 0.260).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.400). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [analysis, geospatial, identify, locate, targets, techniques] vs WIDAI-only terms [appropriate, architectures, classification, computer, detection, estimation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.863 (P=0.875, R=0.851). P exceeds R by 0.023 — the DCWF element's 9 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:15.6%, N:77.0%, C:7.4%). No dominant classification for "Identify, locate, and track targets via geospatial analysis techniques." vs "Knowledge of computer vision task taxonomy, including image classification, obje" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.302 < STS 0.400 (gap -0.098). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [analysis, geospatial, identify] and [appropriate, architectures, classification] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.5%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [analysis, geospatial, identify, locate, targets, techniques]. WIDAI terms: [appropriate, architectures, classification, computer, detection, estimation].
